category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Create and verify the beginner iOS SwiftUI Chat Prototype
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Create and verify the beginner iOS SwiftUI Chat Prototype. Apple does not choose one platform-wide first result, but its Develop in Swift tutorial supplies a bounded beginner iOS route from Xcode and iOS platform installation through a visible SwiftUI Chat Prototype. Prior surface before policy commit: Apple platform and developer outcome not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Current Develop in Swift iOS and SwiftUI tutorial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This official path selects iOS, Swift, SwiftUI, and an app project, which is only one of the platform and template combinations Apple documents.

alternatives_considered.name: Generic Xcode project creation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The guide explicitly requires a platform and template choice such as multiplatform SwiftUI, a macOS command-line tool, iOS augmented reality, or a visionOS immersive environment.

alternatives_considered.name: Device testing or distribution
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Testing and distribution introduce Apple Account, signing, device, membership, and App Store Connect gates beyond a simulator-based local app.

entry_point.developer_intent: Reach a first Apple Developer result without a selected Apple platform, application type, technology, runtime target, or distribution goal.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Apple platform, app template, framework, device or simulator, or distribution intent has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Develop in Swift beginner tutorial ends when the SwiftUI live preview renders the two-bubble Chat Prototype matching Apple's reference ContentView.swift.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The live preview renders both exact messages as padded rounded bubbles, yellow first and teal second, matching the `hello-swiftui--second-bubble-modifiers-ContentView.swift` reference.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The live preview renders both exact messages as padded rounded bubbles, yellow first and teal second, matching the `hello-swiftui--second-bubble-modifiers-ContentView.swift` reference.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The live preview renders both exact messages as padded rounded bubbles, yellow first and teal second, matching the `hello-swiftui--second-bubble-modifiers-ContentView.swift` reference.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Select an Apple platform, project template, interface technology, and local simulation, physical-device, or distribution outcome.
The documented Xcode route requires a compatible Mac that can run the selected current Xcode release.
Install Xcode and the SDK or simulator runtime for the selected platform.
Local learning does not require paid program enrollment; an Apple Account is used for downloads and on-device testing, while distribution requires the Apple Developer Program.

primary_path.action: Choose the target Apple platform, Xcode template and interface, and the intended first result.
details: Apple lists iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, and watchOS, plus App Store, games, accessories, and Safari development.
Xcode templates include materially different app and command-line outcomes.
primary_path.input: Target user device, app or tool type, language and UI preference, available hardware, and whether the goal is simulation, device testing, or distribution.
primary_path.output: A selected Apple official tutorial or project guide.
primary_path.success_signal: No shared runnable result exists at this router.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The remaining path cannot be atomic until the platform and project outcome are chosen.

primary_path.action: Follow the selected guide through tool and SDK installation, project configuration, code or interface changes, build, runtime launch, and observable verification.
details: The beginner iOS candidate starts with Xcode installation and an iOS platform download, then creates an iOS App project using SwiftUI and Swift and continues into the Hello, SwiftUI interface tutorial.
Other platforms and templates change these steps and the terminal signal.
primary_path.input: Human-selected platform, template, toolchain, target, and account state.
primary_path.output: A route-specific first build, preview, simulated app, device app, command-line result, or service result.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected official guide's stated or demonstrated terminal state is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Xcode compatibility, large platform downloads, signing, simulator availability, device trust, template configuration, compile errors, or membership restrictions can block different routes.

primary_path.action: On a compatible Mac, install Xcode from the Mac App Store if it is not already installed.
details: The official tutorial warns that Xcode is a large download and that downloading and installing it may take some time.
Paid Apple Developer Program enrollment is not required for this local learning route.
primary_path.input: Compatible Mac, Mac App Store access, storage, and network connectivity.
primary_path.success_signal: Xcode launches on the Mac.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for the large download and installation. Unsupported macOS, unavailable storage, download, installation, or App Store access problems block the tutorial.

primary_path.action: Launch Xcode, select iOS in the platform selection window, and choose Download & Install.
details: The iOS SDK and simulator components must finish downloading before the project can use the tutorial platform.
primary_path.success_signal: Xcode reports the iOS platform ready.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The platform download can be large; an incomplete or failed download prevents the iOS project flow.

primary_path.action: Launch Xcode, choose Create New Project or File > New > Project, select iOS, select the App template, and select Next.
primary_path.output: New iOS App project configuration form.
primary_path.success_signal: The project options page opens for an iOS App.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting a different platform or template changes the generated files and tutorial route.

primary_path.action: Set Product Name exactly to `ChatPrototype`, set Organization Identifier to `com.<yourname>`, and select Next.
details: The official example organization identifier is `com.sophiesun`.
primary_path.input: Product name `ChatPrototype` and an organization identifier in the documented `com.<yourname>` form.
primary_path.success_signal: Xcode accepts the exact product name and organization identifier and opens the save dialog.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid or different product identity changes the project the tutorial expects.

primary_path.action: Choose a save location and select Create.
primary_path.input: Configured project and writable local folder.
primary_path.output: Generated Xcode project and Swift source files.
primary_path.success_signal: The project opens in the Xcode workspace.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid name, permissions, or storage failure prevents project generation.

primary_path.action: Inspect the generated source and identify element names that use recognizable natural-language terms from Swift and SwiftUI.
details: This is the first of the Hello SwiftUI tutorial's code-orientation actions.
primary_path.output: Recognized Swift and SwiftUI element names in generated code.
primary_path.success_signal: The learner can locate the named code elements in the editor.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the generated source is not visible, reopen the project or its ContentView file before continuing.

primary_path.action: Locate the braces and parentheses in the generated SwiftUI source.
primary_path.output: Identified code grouping and call punctuation.
primary_path.success_signal: Braces and parentheses are located in the source.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Continue inspecting the generated source until both punctuation forms are identifiable.

primary_path.action: Locate the indentation in the generated code and relate it to the source's organization.
primary_path.success_signal: The learner can identify how indentation organizes nested code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Reinspect the generated view structure if the nesting is unclear.

primary_path.action: Inspect syntax highlighting and distinguish the Swift keyword `struct` from the SwiftUI feature `Image`.
details: The tutorial uses the different highlighting of `struct` and `Image` to explain language keywords and framework features.
primary_path.output: Distinguished Swift language and SwiftUI framework tokens.
primary_path.success_signal: The learner identifies `struct` as a Swift keyword and `Image` as a SwiftUI feature.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If syntax coloring is unavailable, use the visible token names and tutorial explanation before continuing.

primary_path.action: Locate the red, quoted `Hello, world!` string in the generated `Text` view.
primary_path.success_signal: The quoted `Hello, world!` text is visible in the editor.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Open the generated ContentView source if the initial Text view is not visible.

primary_path.action: Replace `Hello, world!` with the exact reference value `Knock, knock!`.
primary_path.success_signal: The source and preview show `Knock, knock!`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A misspelled or unquoted value does not match the tutorial reference.

primary_path.action: Delete the entire string and its quotation marks so the code becomes `Text()` and deliberately produces the first compiler error.
details: This error is intentional and must be observed before it is repaired.
primary_path.success_signal: Xcode displays the first error for the empty Text initializer.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If no error appears, confirm that both the string and quotation marks were deleted.

primary_path.action: Type one opening quotation mark inside `Text()`, deleting an auto-inserted closing quote if necessary, so the code becomes `Text(")` without a closing quote.
details: The intended source state is the tutorial's unterminated-string example.
primary_path.output: Text initializer with one unmatched opening quote.
primary_path.success_signal: Xcode marks the unterminated string as an error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If Xcode automatically inserts a closing quote, delete it to produce the documented error state.

primary_path.action: Expand the red error-banner icon, inspect both errors, and confirm the `Unterminated string literal` error.
primary_path.success_signal: The error list includes `Unterminated string literal`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If that diagnostic is absent, restore the unmatched-quote source state and reopen the error banner.

primary_path.action: Add the second quotation mark so the code becomes `Text("")` and clears the unterminated-string error.
primary_path.success_signal: The `Unterminated string literal` error disappears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or misplaced closing quote leaves the compiler error active.

primary_path.action: Restore the exact code `Text("Knock, knock!")`.
primary_path.success_signal: The code compiles and the preview again shows `Knock, knock!`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Quotation, punctuation, or spelling errors keep the code from matching the reference.

primary_path.action: Delete the `Image` line and its next two modifier lines, then create a new line below the remaining Text view.
primary_path.input: Generated VStack with Image and Text content.
primary_path.output: VStack containing the first Text view and an insertion line.
primary_path.success_signal: The Image and both attached modifier lines are gone and the insertion point is below the first Text view.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Deleting the wrong lines can break the VStack and must be undone before continuing.

primary_path.action: Open the Library with View > Show Library or Shift+Command+L.
primary_path.input: Xcode editor with the insertion point below the first Text view.
primary_path.success_signal: The Library appears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the shortcut does not open it, use the View menu command.

primary_path.action: Select the first icon below the Library search field to show Views.
primary_path.success_signal: The Library displays view components.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting another Library category hides the Text view component.

primary_path.action: Search for `text`, select `Text`, and insert it with a double-click or Return.
primary_path.input: Library Views collection and active source insertion point.
primary_path.output: Inserted Text view with selected placeholder content.
primary_path.success_signal: A second Text view appears in the VStack source.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If insertion occurs outside the VStack, move or undo it and insert at the documented line.

primary_path.action: Replace the selected placeholder with the exact reference code `Text("Who's there?")`.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview displays `Who's there?` below the first message.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incorrect apostrophe, quotation, or placement prevents an exact match to the tutorial reference.

primary_path.action: Insert an empty line immediately below the first Text view.
primary_path.input: Two Text views in the VStack.
primary_path.output: Modifier insertion line for the first message.
primary_path.success_signal: The cursor is on an empty line directly below `Text("Knock, knock!")`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A line outside the first Text view's modifier chain will apply modifiers incorrectly.

primary_path.action: Type `.pad`, select `padding(_ edges:_ length:)` in code completion, and press Return to insert `.padding()`.
primary_path.input: Modifier insertion line below the first Text view.
primary_path.success_signal: The modifier compiles and appears directly after the first Text view.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting another completion or placing the modifier elsewhere changes the documented result.

primary_path.action: Inspect the preview and confirm that padding adds space around the first view.
primary_path.success_signal: Visible space surrounds the first message in the preview.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Resume or refresh the preview if the compiled padding change is not visible.

primary_path.action: Below `.padding()`, type `.back`, choose `background(_ style:in:)`, and press Return.
details: The official reference initially shows `.background(ShapeStyle, in: Shape)` placeholders.
primary_path.output: Background modifier with style and shape placeholders.
primary_path.success_signal: The background modifier is inserted below padding.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing a different overload changes the placeholder sequence and tutorial path.

primary_path.action: Replace the first background placeholder with `Color.yellow`, then press Tab to move to the shape argument.
primary_path.output: Yellow background style with selected shape argument.
primary_path.success_signal: `Color.yellow` is the first argument and the shape placeholder is active.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different color or argument position does not match the first bubble reference.

primary_path.action: Choose `RoundedRectangle` with `cornerRadius`, set the radius to `8`, and complete `.background(Color.yellow, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8))`.
primary_path.input: Yellow background modifier with selected shape argument.
primary_path.output: Yellow rounded background on the first message.
primary_path.success_signal: The modifier compiles with `RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8)`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong shape, initializer, radius, punctuation, or argument placement causes a different result or compiler error.

primary_path.action: Reverse the modifier order for the experiment so `.background(...)` precedes `.padding()`.
details: This deliberate experiment demonstrates that SwiftUI modifier order changes the rendered result.
primary_path.input: First Text view with padding followed by yellow background.
primary_path.output: First Text view with yellow background followed by padding.
primary_path.success_signal: The reversed modifier sequence compiles and visibly changes the bubble.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Changing modifier contents instead of only their order invalidates the comparison.

primary_path.action: Select Selectable Mode at the preview's bottom-leading corner.
primary_path.input: Running SwiftUI preview with reversed modifier order.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview accepts view selections.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the control is unavailable, resume the preview and try again.

primary_path.action: Select the top message bubble and confirm the thin blue selection border.
primary_path.success_signal: A thin blue border appears around the selected top bubble.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Select the top bubble again if another preview element receives focus.

primary_path.action: Restore the original modifier order so `.padding()` precedes `.background(Color.yellow, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8))`.
primary_path.input: First Text view with reversed modifiers.
primary_path.success_signal: Padding again appears before the yellow rounded background in source.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Leaving the order reversed does not match the final tutorial reference.

primary_path.action: Inspect the restored modifier sequence and its visible effect in the preview.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected top bubble reflects padding inside its yellow rounded background.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the preview is stale, resume or refresh it before comparing the result.

primary_path.action: Select Live Mode to return the preview to live mode.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview returns to Live Mode.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Resume or refresh the preview if Live Mode does not activate.

primary_path.action: Apply `.padding()` and `.background(Color.teal, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8))` to the second `Text("Who's there?")` bubble and inspect the live preview.
details: The final reference contains a VStack with a yellow `Knock, knock!` bubble and a teal `Who's there?` bubble, each padded with a rounded background, plus the outer VStack padding.
App Store distribution, paid membership, signing for distribution, and physical-device setup occur after this candidate terminal.
primary_path.input: Live preview with completed first bubble and unstyled second Text view.
primary_path.success_signal: The live preview renders both exact messages as padded rounded bubbles, yellow first and teal second, matching the `hello-swiftui--second-bubble-modifiers-ContentView.swift` reference.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Compile, modifier-order, color, shape, radius, text, preview, or runtime errors mean the documented Hello SwiftUI terminal has not been reached.

branches.condition: The current beginner iOS and SwiftUI path is selected.
branches.path: Install Xcode from the Mac App Store, launch it, select iOS in the platform selection window and wait for Download & Install, choose Create New Project, select the App template in the iOS tab, keep the tutorial's SwiftUI, Swift, None testing, and None storage settings, choose a save location and create the project, inspect the generated project and SwiftUI canvas, then continue to Hello, SwiftUI and make the instructed Chat Prototype text, style, and layout changes while confirming the interface in Xcode's preview or simulator.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The first bounded result is a working iOS SwiftUI project with the tutorial's visible interface, not App Store distribution.

branches.condition: A different local Xcode platform or template is selected.
branches.path: Use Create New Project, choose the required platform and template, enter the product and organization configuration Xcode requests, select language, interface, testing and storage options where applicable, save the project, resolve any platform SDK download, then build and run on an appropriate simulator or supported Mac target and verify the generated or tutorial-specific behavior.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Project fields, SDK, source files, runtime target, and observable output differ by platform and template.

branches.condition: Physical-device testing or distribution is the selected outcome.
branches.path: Add an Apple Account to Xcode, configure the team and signing identity, connect and trust an eligible device for testing, or enroll in the Apple Developer Program and follow App Store Connect, archive, upload, and distribution requirements.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Account, signing, device registration, membership, legal, review, and wait gates are added; these are not required for simulator-based learning.

friction_gates.description: Operating-system platform, template, framework, runtime target, and distribution intent must be selected.
Xcode-based development requires compatible Apple hardware and the selected simulator or device support.
Xcode is a large installation, and the beginner tutorial separately downloads and installs the iOS platform runtime.
Project name, organization identifier, interface, language, testing, storage, platform, and run destination vary by template.
An Apple Account and signing configuration become necessary for physical-device and distribution paths.
Paid program enrollment is not required for learning or simulator use, but distribution membership is listed at 99 USD per year where applicable.
Xcode and platform-runtime downloads, builds, simulator boot, and signing can introduce waits before verification.

sources.title: Platforms
evidence_supported: App Store, games, accessories, and Safari alternatives

sources.title: Creating an Xcode project for an app
Hello, SwiftUI tutorial data
sections_used: Add a view from the Library
evidence_supported: All 32 atomic Hello SwiftUI tutorial actions
Exact ChatPrototype identity and text literals
Padding, background, rounded rectangle, and modifier-order experiment
Final yellow and teal message bubbles

uncertainties.question: Which Apple operating-system platform, Xcode template, technology, and terminal result should define the workshop's first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes required hardware, SDK downloads, project configuration, code, signing, runtime, account and payment gates, waits, and verification.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Apple's official platform and Xcode documentation deliberately presents multiple platform and template routes and does not select one Apple-wide canonical first success.

excluded_after_success: Additional views, state, navigation, persistence, and networking
Apple Developer Program enrollment unless distribution is selected
App Store Connect app records, archives, uploads, TestFlight, review, and release
Analytics, crash reporting, Xcode Cloud, and production operations
